calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Continued To Jul-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Angel Riley for Craig Chan.

Ms. Riley stated that she is not expecting anyone to appear and object, that they receive monthly restitution payments from the County, that the payments can last for an indefinite period of time and that the family wants nothing to do with it. Ms. Riley also stated that she believes the Defendant owes the estate about $40,000.00, that the Defendant is required to pay $97.00 a month, that she has reached out to the District Attorney and the Victim's Restitution Department, and that they are not very helpful. The Court noted that Grace Yip signed did not sign a waiver as the Trustee. Ms. Riley stated that she does not think the Court needs to be concerned, that Ms. Yip is the Trustee and a Beneficiary, and that all of the Beneficiaries of the trust have signed waivers. The Court stated that it does not know if the Beneficiaries can waive the rights of the trust without the Trustee waiving the rights. The Second Amended Petition for Instructions and for Settlement of First and Final Account, etc. is continued to July 23, 2019 and will be deemed under submission on that date contingent upon getting a waiver from Ms. Yip as Trustee on file by July 18, 2019. Ms. Riley will provide a form of order that includes fees and adjust distribution to the Heirs.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Continued To Jan-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Angel Riley for (Administrator) Craig Chan. Sean Carberry for and with (objector) Sherman Yee. Vivian Lerche specially appearing for Hos Cooc for (brother) Jackie Yee.

Ms. Lerche stated that she is just here to observe, was hired through an agency to sit in for Mr. Cooc. Ms. Riley reported that there was a declaration filed regarding Jackie Yee's position and that he is in agreement with the other beneficiaries. The Court stated that there are many issues and that nothing can happen until it is known who the heirs are and what is due to them. Ms. Riley confirmed that there have not been discussions between the parties. The Court stated that if the parties are prepared to submit on the papers it can move forward. Ms. Riley stated that they do not believe that Sherman Yee is an heir to this estate. Mr. Carberry stated that they have suggested in their objections and replies that a third party genealogist be brought on, that they do not believe that the Administrator has shown due diligence in searching and that they would request an Evidentiary Hearing if there is to be no genealogical report. The Court asked the parties if they are willing to go to Pro Bono Mediation. Mr. Carberry stated that they are amenable however Ms. Riley stated that she does not have her client's permission to enter into mediation. The Court asked if there is additional evidence and Mr. Carberry indicated that they are waiting on an Immigration Report. Mr. Sherman Yee stated that he has spoken with Ms. Riley regarding the heirs and that there is evidence that they have not been forthright. Both parties agreed that 2 hours would be sufficient for an Evidentiary Hearing. The Status on Close of Estate was continued for an Evidentiary hearing to January 11, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court further stated that the proceedings would start at 9:00 a.m. and finish at 12:00 p.m. with a 15 minute recess, that briefs, witness lists and exhibit lists are to be filed no later than January 4, 2019, that the parties are to meet and confer regarding factual stipulations, proposed exhibits that can be admitted into evidence, to flush out objections and to try and reach a resolution or to narrow the issues. The Court expects that the sole issue is the objectors right to heirship and that all other issues are reserved. The Court also stated that the parties are to think about the other issues such as regarding the half brother Hain Fong that there is not clear or sufficient evidence that he pre or post deceased and if he had any issue, there is no schedule regarding statutory or extraordinary fees and that they need to figure out a way to deal with the continuing stream of income into the estate. The Court confirmed that Ms. Lerche's client does not intend to participate in the Evidentiary Hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Wu. (Not Reported)
